evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Model name 'claude-opus-4-8' is fabricated/non-standard. cache_control is shown as a top-level kwarg to messages.create, which is incorrect — cache_control is placed on content blocks (system blocks, tool definitions, message content), not as a top-level parameter. tool_use roundtrip is mostly right but the cache_control top-level usage is a fabricated API surface.
evidence.executability: Circular import: endpoints.py imports from main, and main imports router from endpoints. cache_control top-level kwarg will raise TypeError/400. DummyStream test mocks AsyncMock(return_value=DummyStream()) won't behave as an async context manager when awaited via 'async with'. Model name is invalid. Multiple non-trivial fixes required.
evidence.completeness: (a) key acquisition mentioned with URL; (b) install bash present; (c) no explicit .env template file with KEY=VALUE block — env vars only referenced in Pydantic Settings class; (d) backend code present; (e) no frontend (FastAPI only, acceptable); (f) test files present but no explicit single test command in a bash block under a Test header. Env-var listed-vs-referenced borderline (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY referenced, no explicit template).
evidence.citation_anchoring: Cites platform.claude.com and docs.anthropic.com-adjacent URLs (platform.claude.com is non-canonical; docs.anthropic.com is canonical). Mix of GitHub repo, support pages, and a dev.to blog. Citations placed inline near code. No fabricated obvious URLs but platform.claude.com domain is suspect vs canonical docs.anthropic.com.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true: applied long-tail handling. Internal inconsistencies (cache_control top-level vs block-level claims in same playbook; fabricated model name 'claude-opus-4-8'; circular import) prevent high score. platform.claude.com appears to be a non-canonical/hallucinated alternative to docs.anthropic.com.
